skill.name: conductor-new-track
skill.description: Create a new track with specification and phased implementation plan
skill.summary: Transform project ideas into structured, trackable implementation plans with detailed specifications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 80 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is pure Markdown documentation describing an AI assistant workflow for creating project specifications and implementation plans. Backtick characters are Markdown formatting, not shell commands. No executable code, network access, or dangerous patterns exist in this skill.

content.value_statement: Transform vague project ideas into detailed specifications with phased implementation plans. Eliminate scope creep and improve project tracking with structured workflows.
actual_capabilities: Classify tasks as feature, bug, chore, or refactor tracks
Guide users through interactive specification gathering with targeted questions
Generate structured specification documents with acceptance criteria
Create phased implementation plans with verification steps
Generate unique track IDs and maintain track registry
Create directory structure with spec, plan, metadata, and index files
limitations: Requires Conductor system to be initialized with product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md
Limited to 6 questions maximum during specification gathering
Only works within the Conductor workflow directory structure
Does not implement the actual project, only creates planning documents

use_cases.title: Feature Development
use_cases.description: Plan new features with clear user stories, acceptance criteria, and phased implementation steps before coding begins.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers starting new feature work

use_cases.title: Bug Tracking
use_cases.description: Document bugs systematically with reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior, and root cause hypotheses.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers and developers fixing bugs

use_cases.title: Project Management
use_cases.description: Create structured specifications and implementation plans for team projects with clear phases and verification criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new feature track for adding user authentication to my web application using JWT tokens.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a clear feature idea and want to start planning

prompt_templates.title: Bug Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bug track for the login form that crashes when users submit empty fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: When documenting a specific bug that needs fixing

prompt_templates.title: Dependency Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chore track for updating all npm dependencies to their latest stable versions.
prompt_templates.scenario: When planning maintenance tasks or dependency updates

prompt_templates.title: Complex Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor track for migrating the codebase from callback-based async to async/await patterns. The refactor should maintain backward compatibility during the transition.
prompt_templates.scenario: When planning significant code improvements with multiple phases

output_examples.input: Create a feature track for user authentication
output: Created conductor/tracks/user-auth_20250115/spec.md with user story and acceptance criteria

output_examples.input: Create a bug track for navigation crash
output: Created bug specification with reproduction steps
Created plan with root cause analysis and fix phases
Added to active tracks list in conductor/index.md

best_practices: Complete all specification questions before generating the implementation plan
Define clear acceptance criteria that are testable and specific
Review and approve the spec before proceeding to plan generation
Break implementation into independently verifiable phases
anti_patterns: Skipping the specification phase and jumping directly to implementation
Writing vague acceptance criteria that cannot be verified
Creating phases that are too large or not independently testable
Ignoring dependency tracking between related tracks

faq.question: What is a track in Conductor?
faq.answer: A track is a structured project unit that includes a specification document, implementation plan, and progress tracking. Tracks can be features, bugs, chores, or refactors.

faq.question: Do I need to set up Conductor before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Run /conductor:setup first to initialize product.md, tech-stack.md, and workflow.md. The skill will check for these files and prompt you if setup is needed.

faq.question: How many questions will the skill ask me?
faq.answer: Maximum 6 questions, asked one at a time. Questions are tailored to your track type (feature, bug, chore, or refactor).

faq.question: Can I edit the generated spec and plan?
faq.answer: Yes. After generation, you can review and request edits before the files are finalized. You can also manually edit the markdown files afterward.

faq.question: What happens if two tracks have the same ID?
faq.answer: The skill automatically detects collisions and appends a counter. For example, user-auth_20250115 becomes user-auth_20250115_2.

faq.question: How do I start implementing a track after creation?
faq.answer: Run /conductor:implement {trackId} to begin the guided implementation process following the phased plan.
